EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NDAWN station at Adams 5N consistently reported wind chills -40 or lower from January 22 through the early morning hours of January 24. The lowest wind chill value during this period at the station was -57.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper trough moved across the Northern Plains, bringing strong winds, light snow, and blizzard conditions. The initial area of light snow ended during the early morning of January 18 as a strong cold front moved into the region. Post frontal snow showers along with strong winds behind the front contributed to widespread whiteout conditions in blowing snow primarily in northeast North Dakota and the Red River Valley of northwest Minnesota. Strongest wind gusts were 50 to 57 mph. Interstate 29 was closed from Grand Forks, ND to the International Border during due to whiteout conditions. A no travel advised was also issued over parts of northeast ND by NDDOT.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Whiteout conditions due to falling snow and blowing snow were reported at KGFK ASOS, with winds gusting 35 to 56 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A clipper-type system brought a period of strong winds and light to locally moderate snow accumulations that resulted in localized blizzard conditions within the Red River Valley of eastern North Dakota.  Snow totals ranged from around inch to 3.1 inches. The highest wind gusts ranged from 45mph to 49 mph for most of eastern North Dakota, with one gust to 58 mph at Grand Forks International Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Off duty NWS employee reported blizzard conditions ongoing in Grand Forks County.
